QF.id: Retirement Planning Products: Other Investment Products_32

question.text: The Post Office Recurring Deposit has a tenure of:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Post Office RD has a fixed tenure of 5 years with minimum monthly deposits of Rs.10 in multiples of Rs.5. Interest is compounded quarterly. **The Trap:** 3 years is available for Post Office Time Deposits but not RD. The RD specifically has only a 5-year option. **Cross-Exam Context:** Post Office RD is tested in Series XVII. Compare with bank RDs which offer various tenures. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Post Office RD has no tax benefits — neither the deposits nor the returns qualify for any tax deduction. For tax efficiency, recommend PPF or ELSS instead.
